message.content: Adversaries may gather DNS information about a victim's network to aid in targeting and reconnaissance. This information can include registered name servers, subdomain addresses, mail servers, and other host details. DNS, MX, TXT, and SPF records can also reveal the use of third-party cloud and SaaS providers like Office 365, G Suite, Salesforce, or Zendesk. Common methods for collecting this data include querying DNS/Passive DNS, searching open technical databases, and accessing online datasets. The gathered information can facilitate further reconnaissance, establish operational resources, and enable initial access through methods such as external remote services. Mitigation strategies include monitoring DNS queries for unusual activity, securing DNS records, and limiting the exposure of DNS information through proper configuration and access controls.
